name: Pork w/ Figs, Pearl Onions & Mustard
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. In a small bowl, combine oil, salt, pepper, and allspice.
3. Rub the pork with the mixture; cover and let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
4. Steep the figs in Marsala wine in a small bowl for 1 hour.
5. Place the onions in warm water for 20 minutes to soften the skins.
6. Using a small knife, peel from tip to stem. Trim the stem as little as possible and cut an X in the root tip. Set aside.
7. Heat oil in a pan over high heat on the stove; brown the pork on all sides. Remove from the heat and transfer to a roasting pan.
8. Add onions, figs, Marsala wine, and broth to the roasting pan. Cover and place in the oven for 1 hour.
9. Remove the cover and continue cooking another 30 minutes, or until a meat thermometer inserted into the pork reads 135°F.
10. Meanwhile, combine honey and mustard in a small condiment bowl.
11. Remove the roasting pan from the oven; transfer the pork to a platter. Let sit for 5 minutes before serving.
12. Add parsley to the onion-fig mixture and beat in butter.
13. To serve, mound the compote on a serving platter. Slice the pork into 1/4-inch slices and lay slices over the compote. Accompany with honey mustard.
